title: Rolling burst illumination for a display
abstract: A display has an array of light emitting elements. For a given frame of a series of frames that present images on the display at a refresh rate of the display, the light emitting elements may be driven by loading individual subsets of the light emitting elements in sequence with light output data, and by illuminating the individual subsets of the light emitting elements in the sequence and in accordance with the light output data, wherein an illumination time period is within a range of about 2% to 80% of a frame time of the frame, the frame time derivable from the refresh rate. This "rolling burst illumination" technique is characterized by the relatively short illumination time period (e.g., as compared to the frame time), and it can stabilize a scene (or mitigate unwanted visual artifacts) for a viewing user during head motion, as well as optimize display bandwidth utilization.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A display comprising: an array of light emitting elements; display driver circuitry coupled to the array of light emitting elements via conductive paths; and one or more controllers to: for a frame of a series of frames that present images on the display at a refresh rate of the display, cause the display driver circuitry to load individual subsets of the light emitting elements in sequence with light output data; and cause the display driver circuitry to illuminate the individual subsets of the light emitting elements in the sequence and in accordance with the light output data, the light emitting elements being illuminated over an illumination time period measured from a time of starting to illuminate a first subset of the light emitting elements to a time of starting to illuminate a last subset of the light emitting elements, wherein the illumination time period of the frame is within a range of about 2% to 80% of a frame time of the frame, the frame time derivable from the refresh rate. 2. The display of claim 1, wherein the one or more controllers are further configured to wait a predefined time period since the first subset of the light emitting elements starts loading with the light output data before causing the display driver circuitry to start illuminating the first subset of the light emitting elements. 3. The display of claim 1, wherein: the one or more controllers are further configured to cause the display driver circuitry to load the individual subsets of the light emitting elements over a loading time period measured from a time of starting to load the first subset of the light emitting elements with the light output data to a time of starting to load the last subset of the light emitting elements with the light output data; and the illumination time period is less than the loading time period. 4. The display of claim 1, wherein the display is a liquid crystal display (LCD), the array of the light emitting elements represents a backlight of the LCD, and the light emitting elements are light emitting diodes (LEDs). 5. The display of claim 1, wherein the display is an organic light emitting diode (OLED) display, the individual light emitting elements in the array of the light emitting elements are light emitting diodes (LEDs) included in individual pixels of the OLED display. 6. The display of claim 1, wherein the display is embedded in a virtual reality (VR) headset or an augmented reality (AR) headset. 7. The display of claim 1, wherein: the array of the light emitting elements is arranged on a substrate that is parallel to a frontal plane of the display in rows and columns; the display driver circuitry includes: first display driver circuitry coupled to odd-numbered rows of the light emitting elements via the conductive paths; and second display driver circuitry coupled to even-numbered rows of the light emitting elements via the conductive paths; causing the display driver circuitry to illuminate the individual subsets of the light emitting elements in the sequence comprises: causing the first display driver circuitry to illuminate the odd-numbered rows of the light emitting elements sequentially; and causing the second display driver circuitry to illuminate the even- numbered rows of the light emitting elements sequentially, wherein, for a pair of an odd-numbered row and an even-numbered row, the one or more controllers cause the second display driver circuitry to start illuminating light emitting elements of the even-numbered row after starting illumination of light emitting elements of the odd-numbered row so that light emitted from the light emitting elements of the even-numbered row fades in while light emitted from the light emitting elements of the odd-numbered row fades out. 8. A method implemented by a display having an array of light emitting elements, the method comprising: for a frame of a series of frames that present images on the display at a refresh rate of the display: loading individual subsets of the light emitting elements sequentially with light output data; and illuminating the individual subsets of the light emitting elements sequentially in accordance with the light output data, the illuminating being performed over an illumination time period measured from a time of starting to illuminate a first subset of the light emitting elements to a time of starting to illuminate a last subset of the light emitting elements, wherein the illumination time period of the frame is within a range of about 2% to 80% of a frame time of the frame, the frame time derivable from the refresh rate. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising waiting a predefined time period since starting the loading before starting the illuminating. 10. The method of claim 8, wherein each individual subset of light emitting elements is illuminated once, not multiple times, per frame. 11. The method of claim 8, wherein: the loading is performed over a loading time period measured from a time of starting to load the first subset of the light emitting elements with the light output data to a time of starting to load the last subset of the light emitting elements with the light output data; and the illumination time period is less than the loading time period. 12. The method of claim 8, wherein the illumination time period of the frame is no greater than about 1/3 of the frame time. 13. The method of claim 8, wherein the refresh rate is at least about 75 hertz (Hz) and the illumination time period of the frame is no greater than about 3 milliseconds (ms). 14. The method of claim 8, wherein: the array of the light emitting elements is arranged on a substrate that is parallel to a frontal plane of the display in rows and columns; the individual subsets of the light emitting elements represent individual rows of the light emitting elements; and the illuminating the individual subsets of the light emitting elements sequentially comprises illuminating each row of the light emitting elements in sequence starting with a first row of the light emitting elements and ending with a last row of the light emitting elements. 15. The method of claim 8, wherein: the array of the light emitting elements is arranged on a substrate that is parallel to a frontal plane of the display in rows and columns; the illuminating the individual subsets of the light emitting elements sequentially comprises: illuminating odd-numbered rows of the light emitting elements sequentially; and illuminating even-numbered rows of the light emitting elements sequentially, wherein, for a pair of an odd-numbered row and an even-numbered row, light emitting elements of the even-numbered row start illuminating after starting illumination of light emitting elements of the odd-numbered row so that light emitted from the light emitting elements of the even-numbered row fades in while light emitted from the light emitting elements of the odd-numbered row fades out. 16. The method of claim 8, wherein: the individual subsets of the light emitting elements represent individual light emitting elements of the light emitting elements; and the illuminating the individual subsets of the light emitting elements sequentially comprises illuminating each light emitting element of the light emitting elements in sequence starting with a first light emitting element and ending with a last light emitting element. 17. A display comprising: an array of light sources; display driver circuitry coupled to the array of light sources via conductive paths; and one or more controllers to: for a frame of a series of frames that present images on the display, cause the display driver circuitry to load individual subsets of the light sources in sequence with light output data, the light sources being loaded with the light output data over a loading time period measured from a time of starting to load a first subset of the light sources with the light output data to a time of starting to load a last subset of the light sources with the light output data; and cause the display driver circuitry to illuminate the individual subsets of the light sources in the sequence and in accordance with the light output data, the light sources being illuminated over an illumination time period measured from a time of starting to illuminate the first subset of the light sources to a time of starting to illuminate the last subset of the light sources, wherein the illumination time period is less than the loading time period. 18. The display of claim 17, wherein: the series of frames present the images on the display at a refresh rate of the display; and the illumination time period of the frame is within a range of about 2% to 80% of a frame time of the frame, the frame time derivable from the refresh rate. 19. The display of claim 17, wherein: the array of the light sources is arranged on a substrate that is parallel to a frontal plane of the display in rows and columns; the individual subsets of the light sources represent individual rows of the light sources; and the illuminating the individual subsets of the light sources sequentially comprises illuminating each row of the light sources in sequence starting with a first row of the light sources and ending with a last row of the light sources. 20. The display of claim 17, wherein: the conductive paths are arranged in horizontal lines and vertical lines on a substrate that is parallel to a frontal plane of the display; and the display driver circuitry is configured address an individual light source of the light sources via a pair of a horizontal line and a vertical line that intersects at the individual light source for loading the light output data that is particular to the individual light source.
